prediction_reason.历年位次与招生计划: 2021–2025年吉林省理科/物理类最低录取位次分别为约256名→397名→214名→423名→498名，整体呈现波动上升趋势（位次数字增大），近两年连续后移，2025年基准位次约498名；该校在吉林省理科/物理类招生计划2021–2024年均为11人，2025年增至12人，2026年进一步扩至13人，较2025年扩招约8.3%，招生名额增加对竞争有一定缓解作用。
prediction_reason.学校层次与声誉: 北京大学医学部为985、211、双一流建设高校，隶属北京大学体系，软科主榜北京大学位列第2名，属全国顶尖层次，对高分考生吸引力极强；北京大学（校本部）2026年声誉评价偏负面，但北京大学医学部未出现独立负面事件；抖音热度方面北京大学总排名第3（高热），医学部作为其重要组成部分享有同等品牌效应；历史投档异常记录中未见北京大学医学部在吉林省出现断档信号；临床医学（A）、口腔医学（A）、基础医学（A）、药学（A+）、护理学（A+）、公共卫生与预防医学（A）等均为A-/A/A+等级，学科实力顶尖，对医学类考生有持续吸引力。
prediction_reason.专业热度与地域: 临床医学（2024年热度70.19，推荐）、口腔医学（72.31，推荐）等医学类专业持续高热；吉林省2026年普通高考总人数约110868人，物理类估算约80000人，较2025年80421人略有减少，竞争略有缓解；2026年；北京大学医学部位于北京，属跨省招生，吉林省考生对北京高校有较强地域偏好；该校在吉林省招生计划近年稳定在11–13人，占比极小，属典型的外省顶尖高校招生模式。
prediction_reason.预测结论: 综合2025年基准位次498名、2026年扩招至13人、考生规模略减、医学专业热度持续等因素，预计2026年最低录取位次在523名基础上小幅上移至约538名，区间473–603名。扩招带来的位次后移效应与考生规模略减形成对冲，医学类专业的持续高热使竞争保持稳定偏紧态势。

major_tier_recommendation.tier_1:
一档（最难进）
- 口腔医学（学制8年）（八年制本博连读）
- 临床医学（学制8年）（八年制本博连读）
- 基础医学（学制8年）（八年制本博连读）。
major_tier_recommendation.tier_2:
二档（中等）
- 口腔医学（学制5年）
- 药学（学制6年）（六年制本硕连读）
- 临床医学（学制5年）。
major_tier_recommendation.tier_3:
三档（相对好进）
- 预防医学（学制7年）（七年制本硕连读）。
major_tier_recommendation.overall_advice: 位次在预测区间473–603名内，建议以二档专业为主力填报、一档专业冲刺、三档专业保底。位次明显优于区间下限（473名以内）可重点冲击八年制本博连读专业；位次接近区间上限（603名附近）应以三档预防医学及二档五年制临床/口腔为主，确保录取。

历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plan
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum admission rankings for science/physics category in Jilin Province were approximately 256 → 397 → 214 → 423 → 498, showing an overall fluctuating upward trend (increasing ranking numbers), with consecutive declines in the last two years. The baseline ranking for 2025 is about 498. The enrollment plan for science/physics category in Jilin Province was 11 students from 2021 to 2024, increased to 12 in 2025, and further expanded to 13 in 2026, an increase of about 8.3% compared to 2025. The increase in enrollment slots has a certain alleviating effect on competition.

学校层次与声誉.title: School Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: Peking University Health Science Center is a 985, 211, and Double First-Class university, affiliated with Peking University system. Peking University ranks 2nd on the Soft Science main list, belonging to the top national tier, with strong appeal to high-scoring candidates. The reputation evaluation of Peking University (main campus) in 2026 is somewhat negative, but Peking University Health Science Center has no independent negative incidents. In terms of Douyin popularity, Peking University ranks 3rd overall (high heat), and the Health Science Center, as an important component, enjoys the same brand effect. No historical abnormal admission records show any signal of admission gaps for Peking University Health Science Center in Jilin Province. Disciplines such as Clinical Medicine (A), Stomatology (A), Basic Medicine (A), Pharmacy (A+), Nursing (A+), and Public Health and Preventive Medicine (A) are all rated A-/A/A+, with top-tier academic strength, providing sustained appeal to medical candidates.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: Clinical Medicine (2024 popularity 70.19, recommended), Stomatology (72.31, recommended), and other medical majors remain highly popular. The total number of candidates for the 2026 Jilin Province general college entrance examination is approximately 110,868, with an estimated 80,000 in the physics category, a slight decrease from 80,421 in 2025, leading to a slight easing of competition. In 2026, Peking University Health Science Center is located in Beijing, involving cross-province enrollment, and Jilin candidates have a strong regional preference for Beijing universities. The enrollment plan for this university in Jilin Province has been stable at 11–13 students in recent years, accounting for a very small proportion, typical of top-tier out-of-province university enrollment models.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the 2025 baseline ranking of 498, the 2026 expansion to 13 students, a slight decrease in candidate numbers, and sustained popularity of medical majors, the minimum admission ranking for 2026 is expected to slightly shift upward from 523 to about 538, with a range of 473–603. The effect of ranking decline due to expansion is offset by the slight reduction in candidate numbers, while the sustained high popularity of medical majors keeps competition stable and relatively tight.

major_tier_recommendation_en.tier_1:
Tier 1 (Most Difficult to Enter)
- Stomatology (8-year program) (Eight-year combined bachelor's and doctoral program)
- Clinical Medicine (8-year program) (Eight-year combined bachelor's and doctoral program)
- Basic Medicine (8-year program) (Eight-year combined bachelor's and doctoral program).
major_tier_recommendation_en.tier_2:
Tier 2 (Moderate)
- Stomatology (5-year program)
- Pharmacy (6-year program) (Six-year combined bachelor's and master's program)
- Clinical Medicine (5-year program).
major_tier_recommendation_en.tier_3:
Tier 3 (Relatively Easy to Enter)
- Preventive Medicine (7-year program) (Seven-year combined bachelor's and master's program).
major_tier_recommendation_en.overall_advice: For rankings within the predicted range of 473–603, it is recommended to focus on Tier 2 majors as the main choice, aim for Tier 1 majors as a stretch, and use Tier 3 majors as a safety. Candidates with rankings significantly better than the lower limit (within 473) can focus on the eight-year combined bachelor's and doctoral programs; those with rankings near the upper limit (around 603) should prioritize Tier 3 Preventive Medicine and Tier 2 five-year Clinical/Stomatology programs to ensure admission.
